name: Flourless Chocolate Espresso Cake
recipeIngredient: 3 packages Frozen raspberries in syrup (Thawed/10 oz each)
1 tablespoon Instant espresso powder dissolved in 1 cup Water
recipeInstructions:
For Sauce: Working in batches, puree raspberries and syrup in a processor. Strain the puree into a medium bowl and chill.
For Cake:
1. Preheat the oven to 350°F (175°C).
2. Line the bottom of a 9-inch-diameter cake pan with parchment paper, ensuring it overhangs by at least 2 inches on all sides.
3. Place all chocolate pieces in a large bowl.
4. In a medium saucepan, bring butter, espresso and sugar to a boil, stirring to dissolve the sugar. Remove from heat and add to the chocolate; whisk until smooth. Allow to cool slightly.
5. Whisk in eggs.
6. Pour the batter into the prepared cake pan.
7. Place the cake pan in a larger roasting pan and pour hot water into the larger pan until it reaches halfway up the sides of the cake pan.
8. Bake until the center of the cake is set and a tester inserted into the center comes out with a few moist crumbs attached, about 1 hour. Remove the pan from the water.
9. Allow the cake to cool completely before serving.
10. To serve, invert the cake onto a platter, holding the bottom of the pan over low heat for 15 seconds to release it slightly. Place the platter over the pan and hold both tightly. Invert carefully to lift off the cake pan and parchment paper. Serve with sauce and fresh raspberries.
